purpose: Describes Synapse's circuit-breaker mechanism that pauses an in-flight swarm and triggers replanning when failure patterns indicate a planning error. Triggers include 3+ related failures in the same wave, cascading failures from a blocking task, or worker deviations that invalidate plan assumptions. The breaker prevents cascading damage and forces the master to reassess.
gotchas: Don't pause for isolated failures -- retry or route around them; circuit breaker is for systemic problems
Resumption after circuit break requires fresh planning, not just retrying the same dispatch
conventions: Master logs the breaker trip with the triggering condition before pausing
